data.title: The Now Corporation launches joint venture with Chronical Engineering
data.description:
• The Now Corporation (OTCPK:NWPN), through its subsidiary Green Rain Solar, has entered into a new joint venture with Chronical Engineering, the company said on Tuesday.
  • The joint venture will focus on the construction and leasing of multiple solar farms and EV charging stations.
  • The company plans to begin construction on three initial sites in Orange County, with potential expansion into additional markets based on performance and demand.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Chartwell Retirement Residences declares $0.051 dividend
data.description:
• Chartwell Retirement Residences (CSH.UN:CA) declares $0.051/share monthly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.8%
  • Payable March 17; for shareholders of record Feb. 28; ex-div Feb. 28
  • See CSH.UN:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Premier enters $200M accelerated share repurchase program
data.description:
• Premier (NASDAQ:PINC) has entered into accelerated share repurchase agreements with JPMorgan Chase Bank, National Association to repurchase an aggregate of $200M of shares of its Class A common stock.
  • The ASR program was authorized under the company’s $1B, share repurchase authorization approved by the company’s board in February 2024.
  • The company will make a payment of $200M to JPMorgan, and by February 19, 2025, will receive from JPMorgan initial deliveries of ~9M shares of common stock, or $160M worth, based on the closing price on February 14, 2025.
  • The final settlement of the ASR program is expected to be completed no later than Q1 2026.
  • The company is funding the share repurchases using borrowings under its existing credit facility.
  • The company has repurchased $600M of shares of its common stock through a $400M accelerated share repurchase transaction completed in July 2024 and $200M of open market repurchases completed in January 2025.
  • PINC shares rise 2% premarket on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Snowflake upgraded to Outperform on improving trends, enterprise AI: Wolfe
data.description:
Snowflake was upgraded to Outperform from Peer Perform by Wolfe Research ahead of the cloud-based data platform's fourth quarter financial results.

"The foundation of our conviction resides in the combination of our checks clearly pointing towards stabilizing, and even improving, consumption trends and the numbers in recent quarters backing that up," said Wolfe analysts, led by Alex Zukin, in an investor note. "We believe 4Q will be solid with growth in the 28% range, and we see an FY26 product revenue guidance of 23% Y/Y, setting the stage for mid- to-high 20% growth for the year."

Snowflake is slated to release its fourth quarter fiscal 2025 financial results after the U.S. markets close on Wednesday, Feb. 26. A consensus estimate calls for adjusted earnings per share of $0.18 on revenue of $955.79M.

Wolfe expects Snowflake to deliver full-year fiscal 2026 product revenue of $4.26B, which is 1.6% more than the consensus.

"Checks on SNOW this quarter indicated positive consumption momentum and improving experimentation volume around AI," Zukin noted. "Anecdotal conversations suggest that large enterprise customers still view SNOW as the emerging vendor of record. Most interestingly, our data points suggest that incremental positivity for SNOW is not coming at the expense of Databricks but rather a "rising tide" feeling around cloud data migrations."

Wolfe set a $235 price target on Snowflake which implies an EV/CY26E Revenue multiple of 15.9x on its base case estimates.

Snowflake shares have climbed 21.5% year to date and 66% over the past six months.

data.title: Digital Realty raised to Sector Outperform at Scotiabank after recent slide
data.description:
Scotiabank Global Equity Research downgraded Digital Realty Trust (NYSE:DLR) shares to Sector Outperform from Sector Perform on the view that the stock's recent pullback offers an attractive entry point for investors amid still-strong demand for computational power.

"Demand for computational power remains strong as shown by the company’s order book and commentaries by leading hyperscalers in their most recent conference calls," analyst Maher Yaghi wrote in a note to clients.

Since the start of the year, shares of the data center REIT retreated 7.4%, compared with the S&P 500's 4% rise. The stock climbed 31% in 2024.

While Digital Realty's (NYSE:DLR) 2025 AFFO-per-share growth is expected to be pressured by its capital recycling program, "the ongoing capacity build and conversion of bookings to billings should lead to a stronger run rate in FFO and AFFO/share growth in 2026/2027," he noted.

Scotiabank's Market Outperform rate diverges from the SA Quant system rating and the average SA analyst rating, both at Hold, but aligns with the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

DLR gained 1.2% in Tuesday premarket trading.

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher acquires Massachusetts-based Agilis Partners
data.description:
• Insurance broker Arthur J. Gallagher (NYSE:AJG) announced on Tuesday the acquisition of Waltham, Massachusetts-based Agilis Partners.
  • Agilis is an investment and retirement plan consulting firm.
  • The terms of the acquisition were not disclosed.
  • AJG shares were trading +0.26% pre-market at $322.33.

data.title: OppFi increases existing revolving credit facility with affiliates of Blue Owl Capital to $300M
data.description:
• OppFi (NYSE:OPFI) increased the existing revolving credit facility among one of the company’s subsidiaries and affiliates of Blue Owl Capital to $300M, up from $250M.
  • Blue Owl acquired Atalaya Capital Management in September 2024.
  • OppFi increased the total capacity under this revolving credit facility from $250M to $300M.
  • The amended agreement extends the maturity date to February 2029, increases funds available for growth, and provides the company the flexibility to use balance sheet capital to extinguish corporate debt ahead of schedule.
  • OPFI shares rise 1.4% premarket on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: QHSLab sees Q4 revenue to surge 98% YOY
data.description:
• QHSLab (OTCQB:USAQ) on Tuesday said it expects revenue to surge 98% year-over-year to $625,981, compared to $315,020 in Q4 2023.
  • The company expects net income for Q4 2024 of $40,838, while preliminary gross profit increased 117% to $412,154, with gross margin expanding to 65.8%, up from 60.3% in the prior-year quarter.
  • FY24 revenue is expected to grow 51% year-over-year, reaching $2.1 million compared to $1.4 million for the FY23.
  • The company reported preliminary net positive cash flow of $105,586, driven by $247,317 in cash flow from operations, reflecting strong business performance.
  • The company repaid $298,531 in loans.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: IPO Roundup: Anbio Biotechnology, Wellgistics Health and more
data.description:
The U.S. IPO activity looks sluggish amid the holiday-shortened week. Nevertheless, the week prior saw Texas-based cybersecurity firm SailPoint price its upsized IPO of 60M shares at a public offering price of $23.00 per share.

Additionally, shares of defense contractor Karman Holdings (KRMN) saw their shares rise by nearly 50% after its public offering, as investors liked the positioning of this missile, defense & space business.

This week, Germany-based Anbio Biotechnology (NNNN) is expected to raise capital during its IPO.

"NNNN is seeking US public capital market investment as it aims to commercialize its in vitro diagnostics products," said SA Investing Group Leader Donovan Jones in a recent article on Anbio Biotechnology.

"The market opportunity for providing IVD products worldwide is large and is expected to grow at a moderate rate of growth in the coming years."

Upcoming IPOs/SPACs that are expected to be priced this week

  • Anbio Biotechnology (NNNN) is raising $8.8M by offering 1.6M shares at a price range of about $5.00 and $6.00. Description: Medical technology company.
  • Wellgistics Health (WGRX) is raising $4.5M by offering 0.9M shares at a price range of about $4.50 and $5.50. Description: Holding company.
  • Apimeds Pharmaceuticals (APUS) is raising $11.7M by offering 3.3M shares at a price range of about $3.00 and $4.00. Description: Clinical biopharmaceutical company.

Filing, terms, and changes

  • Crane Harbor Acquisition (CHACU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $175M in an initial public offering. Description: Blank check company.
  • Envoy Technologies (EVOY) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $12M in an initial public offering. Description: Electric car-sharing firm.
  • Harvard Ave Acquisition (HAVAU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $250M in an initial public offering. Description: Blank check company.
  • UY Scuti Acquisition (UYSCU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $50M in an initial public offering. Description: Blank check company.
  • Kestra Medical Technologies (KMTS) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $100M in an initial public offering. Description: Wearable cardiac device maker.
  • Robot Consulting (LAWR) has filed and set terms with the SEC to raise up to $13M in an initial public offering. The company plans to offer about 2.5M ADSs priced between $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Japanese HR platform provider.
  • Qinhui Technology International (QHT) has filed and set terms with the SEC to raise up to $7M in an initial public offering. The company plans to offer about 1.3M shares priced between $5.00 and $6.00. Description: Chinese auto parts maker.

Withdrawals

  • Turo has withdrawn its plans for an initial public offering. Previously, the company had filed in 2022 with the SEC to raise about $300M.

Lock-up period expirations

  • Reitar Logtech (RITR) on 2/19/2025. Price movement over the past six months: XX%
  • WORK Medical Technology Group (WOK) on 2/19/2025. Price movement over the past six months: XX%

Since the start of the year, the Renaissance IPO ETF (NYSEARCA:IPO) has risen about 7.1%, outperforming the S&P 500 index, which has gained about 4.0%.

data.title: Cargojet reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Cargojet press release (OTCPK:CGJTF): Q4 Total revenue was C$293.2M compared to fourth quarter 2023 revenue of C$221.9M.
  • Q4 Adjusted EPS C$1.71
